mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is persistent across all macro regimes, making it an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with the 3-month change in INDPRO (Spearman rho < 0.1) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates severe labor market slack and recessionary conditions that depress consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 8.0, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the median return is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low federal funds rates (below 1%) and rising inflation (CPI YoY above 2%) produces negative real interest rates. This reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and boosts valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the joint trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward return is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10), or returns are negative in over 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During episodes of high and accelerating inflation (CPI YoY > 3% and MoM rising for 3 months), strong housing starts signal an overheating economy that forces the Fed to tighten policy. This preemptive monetary response leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and CPIAUCSL MoM accelerating over 3 months
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is active, higher HOUST values are associated with positive 20-day forward returns (cross-sectional rank correlation > 0.1), contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales reflect robust consumer demand, which drives higher corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is always on and provides a persistent bullish indicator.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not positively correlated with the 3-month growth rate of RSAFS (Spearman rho < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, it signals an aggressive easing stance by the central bank, typically in response to economic weakness. Markets interpret this as a powerful accommodative policy that lifts asset prices over the ensuing week.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Within 5 trading days after the trigger, the S&P 500 average return is not positive and the proportion of positive returns does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong gains in payroll employment (PAYEMS MoM > 200k) and retail sales (RSAFS MoM > 0.5%) signal a synchronized expansion in the labor market and consumer spending. This dual confirmation boosts confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both threshold conditions are met, the 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) and the median return is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where GDP is contracting (negative quarter-over-quarter growth for two consecutive quarters), an upturn in industrial production signals early recovery in manufacturing. This rebound anticipation drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq negative for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: When GDP is contracting, a 1% rise in INDPRO is associated with negative 60-day forward returns (rolling 2-year rank correlation < -0.1), refuting the rebound signal.
